danger_signals.visual_form: CPU-only boss characters (Billy, Axel, Laurence, Geese, Krauser) appear after defeating regular roster
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; progression from standard opponents to enhanced difficulty opponents signals increased danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Ryo Sakazaki appears as final opponent after winning all matches in two rounds
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate challenge state; secret boss signals highest difficulty tier and special threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Italy stage and other international locations with detailed environmental elements
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental storytelling through stage design communicates different threat contexts and competitive venues

safety_signals.visual_form: 15 playable characters available (regular roster + 4 former bosses + 3 legacy characters)
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and choice signal safety; diversity of options reduces perceived threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Three characters from original Fatal Fury (Tung Fu Rue, Duck King, Geese Howard) return
safety_signals.meaning: Narrative continuity and established character presence signals reliability and player familiarity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cross-over with Ryo Sakazaki (Art of Fighting protagonist) as hidden opponent
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ryo Sakazaki uses Art of Fighting fighting style distinct from Fatal Fury roster
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: International tournament stages (Italy emphasized) signal global competitive arena

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Progression from playable characters to AI-only bosses to secret boss Ryo
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser, Geese Howard tier distinctly from regular characters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss encounters trigger after defeating 10 regular opponents, creating spatial/progression-based faction separation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight regular characters from previous game visible in roster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can choose first opponent, signaling exploratory world state with agency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tung and Duck appear as 9th-10th opponents after regular roster clearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty increases; legacy character integration signals deeper tournament progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Billy, Axel, Laurence, Geese, Krauser appear sequentially after 10 opponents defeated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced encounter sequence; former boss characters now playable but present as challenges, indicating world-state shift to high-stakes finals

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ryo Sakazaki triggered after winning all matches in two rounds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Secret boss unlocks; cross-series appearance signals exceptional world state (narrative expansion into Art of Fighting universe)

environmental_storytelling.example: Italy stage explicitly shown in gameplay screenshots; multiple international locations suggest tournament has global scope and competitive prestige
Mai Shiranui and Andy Bogard featured in promotional materials showing character-specific visual identities and fighting styles that communicate their narrative roles
New combo system and increased Line Move Attacks communicate mechanical world evolution; faster game speed signals more intense competitive environment
Mai's character design altered in international versions (reduced thong, removed bounce animation) signals regional cultural context and world-state differences across markets

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: 'Nippon Ichi!' voice clip and stance; animation altered in international versions showing regional behavioral adaptation
'Bloody Spin' voice clip retained despite name censorship in international versions; voice communication persists despite visual localization

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Boss characters (Billy, Axel, Laurence, Geese, Krauser)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Transition from AI-only to playable; behavioral flexibility indicates world-state change from antagonist-only to selectable roster
Appears only after perfect tournament run; presence signals cross-series narrative event and exceptional achievement state

notes: Fatal Fury Special communicates world-state primarily through tournament progression structure rather than environmental visual cues. Danger escalation is signaled through character roster tier changes (regular → legacy → boss → secret boss). Faction identity is implicit through character fighting styles and cross-series integration (Art of Fighting). Regional differences (censorship, character roster) indicate world-state variations across market contexts. The game uses character availability and encounter sequencing as primary manifestation tools rather than explicit visual danger indicators. Combo system upgrade and increased game speed communicate mechanical environmental evolution.
